pattern: Replacing a number by its digit sum is a pure per-element transform, so the answer is just the smallest digit sum seen across the array.
hints: Each element is transformed independently -- you never need to look at more than one number at a time.
Write a small helper that reduces a positive integer to the sum of its decimal digits.
Apply the digit-sum helper to every element and return the minimum of the results.

edge_inputs.description: several powers of ten all reduce to digit sum 1, tests correct tie handling
different numbers share the same digit sum
the largest raw number is not the one with the largest digit sum

stress.description: random array across the full length and value ranges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
